name: HashiCorp Vault API
what_it_does: HashiCorp Vault provides a unified API for secrets management, dynamic credential generation, data encryption, and identity-based access — supporting token, AppRole, Kubernetes, AWS IAM, and many other auth methods.
use_cases: Dynamically generate short-lived database credentials for an agent before executing a query, then revoke them after
Store and retrieve application secrets (API keys, TLS certs) in a namespaced secret path using the KV v2 engine
Authenticate a Kubernetes pod via the Vault K8s auth method and obtain a scoped token for downstream API calls
Issue short-lived PKI certificates on demand for mTLS between microservices orchestrated by an agent
Rotate AWS IAM credentials automatically by calling the AWS secrets engine and distributing the new keys
not_for: Storing large binary blobs or files — Vault is optimized for small structured secrets, not object storage
General-purpose database or message queue use cases — it is specifically a secrets and identity broker
Teams without operational capacity to run and maintain Vault infrastructure unless using HCP Vault
best_when: An agent needs to broker secrets, credentials, or encryption keys across multiple systems with fine-grained, auditable access control and dynamic secret generation.
avoid_when: Your team cannot manage Vault infrastructure and does not want to pay for HCP Vault — simpler managed alternatives like AWS Secrets Manager may be more appropriate.

auth.notes: Vault uses its own token system. AppRole is the recommended method for machine-to-machine auth; Kubernetes and AWS IAM auth are preferred for cloud-native agents. All tokens have configurable TTLs and policies.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open-source Vault is free to self-host with no feature limits on core functionality; HCP Vault Dedicated starts at ~$0.03/hr for dev cluster
pricing.paid_tiers: HCP Vault Dedicated (Plus/Standard) and Enterprise on-prem with DR replication, namespaces, HSM support
pricing.notes: Self-hosted open-source is entirely free. HCP Vault (managed) requires a HashiCorp Cloud Platform account with billing.

performance.rate_limits: No hard API rate limits in open-source; HCP Vault enforces request-per-second limits depending on tier; performance replication can scale read throughput

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Vault returns structured JSON errors with HTTP status codes; permission denied errors clearly state the missing policy path, aiding automated diagnosis.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: KV writes are idempotent with CAS (check-and-set) option; lease renewals and token lookups are naturally idempotent; dynamic secret generation is not idempotent by design.
known_agent_gotchas: Token TTL expiry mid-session will cause 403 errors — agents must proactively renew or re-authenticate before expiry
KV v1 and KV v2 have different API paths (secret/ vs secret/data/) — mixing them silently returns wrong data
Lease IDs for dynamic secrets must be tracked and renewed separately from the auth token
Namespace paths differ between open-source (no namespaces) and Enterprise — scripts written for one may fail on the other
AppRole secret_id is single-use by default; agents must generate a new one or configure use_limit > 1

security_score_components.security_notes: Vault is purpose-built for secrets security; supports seal/unseal with HSM or cloud KMS, audit logging to file/syslog/socket, and fine-grained HCL policies. TLS is mandatory in production configurations.
